summary: Comprehensive backend + frontend testing of the new Intercloud client portal (WHMCS-style). Backend: 29/29 pytest cases passing after seed backfill fix. Frontend: found and FIXED a critical navigation bug that blocked 3/5 role scenarios (staff roles were being redirected to /portal/client/dashboard causing infinite bounce/blank screen). After fix, all role-based sidebar filtering verified correctly, landing/legal pages verified, and client happy-path (dashboard→invoices→modal with bank details) verified. Some UI micro-flows (Add Integration dialog native select interaction, Webmail nav) were partially blocked by test-script overlay issues but underlying backend behaviour is proven.

minor.issue: Pre-existing demo client user was not backfilled with billing_emails=['finance@contoh-digital.co.id'] because the idempotent seed only updated password on the elif branch. FIXED in /app/backend/portal/seed.py — now also backfills billing_emails when empty.

ui_bugs.issue: CRITICAL: Non-admin staff roles (sales/support/ticket_only) were routed to /portal/client/dashboard on login, which the RequireAuth guard (role='client') rejects, causing an infinite redirect/blank screen. FIXED — both files now route by 'client vs staff' (staff includes admin/sales/support/ticket_only → /portal/admin/dashboard).

Bank account numbers 1240011911816 / 4730862038 are only rendered inside the pay==='bank' branch (default is null). User must click the bank tile to reveal them. Bank/BCA labels appear as tile description, but full account details need explicit tile click. Consider defaulting pay='bank' or always rendering bank details.

action_items: Verify the two files I edited (PortalLogin.jsx, ProtectedRoute.jsx, seed.py) match your intended nav model.
Consider making the ClientInvoices modal default to the bank-transfer tab so 'MANDIRI 1240011911816 / BCA 4730862038' are visible without extra click.

Do a manual smoke of Admin Integrations Add-Server dialog + Webmail — my playwright script couldn't fully drive them (native <select> option timing + modal overlay intercepting nav clicks); the backend CRUD + test-connection is fully covered by pytest and passes.
critical_code_review_comments: SEC: JWT_SECRET is checked-in in backend/.env (a7f3b219...). Fine for preview, must be rotated for production.
MODEL: server.py routes.py imports `db` at request time via `_get_db()` — good pattern; keeps FastAPI startup order intact.
RBAC: OrderCreateIn (client/orders) doesn't check ownership on read-back — client can only see their own orders because filter is on user_id. OK.
MEMORY: The mock_test_connection uses random.randint each call — non-deterministic but that's expected for a mock.
DRY: PortalLogin.jsx and ProtectedRoute.jsx duplicated the 'admin? admin : client' rule — both had to be patched. Consider extracting a `dashboardFor(role)` helper in portal/api.js.
PERF: /admin/dashboard runs 6+ count_documents + finds when role is admin — fine for demo scale; consider aggregation later.

success_rate.frontend: ~85% (all role RBAC + landing/legal + client dashboard/invoice list verified; Add-Integration Radix/native-select and Webmail nav flows only partially driven by script)

seed_data_creation: Backend backfill will run on next backend restart (already restarted once during testing). No additional seed data created outside standard flows.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest is stable and idempotent-ish (uses TEST_ prefix, tolerates >= counts). RBAC verified end-to-end. Two known-good frontend sub-flows still worth extra manual browser check: (1) AdminIntegrations Add-Server dialog — the module <select> is native HTML (tag=SELECT); use page.select_option with the visible label 'MikroTik RouterOS'. Wait ~1s after dialog open for modules registry API to populate options. Fields render dynamically per int-field-{key}. (2) AdminMail — Inbox lazy-seeds 4 messages on first GET; nav-mail requires no leftover modal on-screen (close any prior dialog with ESC).
rca of the issue: CRITICAL BUG RCA (nav loop): (a) reproduction — login as support/sales/ticket_only from /portal/login. (b) root cause — PortalLogin.jsx submit() and ProtectedRoute.jsx RequireAuth both had a binary check `role === 'admin' ? admin_dash : client_dash`, mistaking sales/support/ticket_only as client. RequireAuth on /portal/client checks role='client', mismatch, bounce back to client dashboard → infinite loop → blank page. (c) mitigation — inverted the check to `role === 'client' ? client_dash : admin_dash` in both files. All 4 admin/staff roles now land correctly on /portal/admin/dashboard and see the sidebar filtered per ADMIN_NAV_GROUPS.roles.
